name: Found Credential - Test Against Login Services

suggestion.description: A credential for user '{trigger.1.name}' was found. An open login service ('{trigger.2.name}') was also discovered. Attempt to reuse the credential.
suggestion.rationale: Credential reuse is a highly effective attack. Credentials found on one system or in one context (e.g., a web config file) should be tested against all other available login services (e.g., SSH, RDP, SMB).
commands: For SSH/FTP/SMB/RDP/WinRM, try direct login or password spraying tools.
For HTTP/HTTPS, look for a web login form at http://{trigger.2.host}:{trigger.2.port}/ and try these credentials.
If the credential is a hash ('{trigger.1.attributes.hash}'), attempt Pass-the-Hash attacks for services like SMB and WinRM.

name: Credential Reuse on Discovered Web Login Page

suggestion.description: A credential for user '{trigger.1.name}' was found, and a potential web login page '{trigger.3.name}' was discovered on the web server.
suggestion.rationale: Testing known credentials against discovered web application login portals is a high-impact technique for gaining initial access or escalating privileges.
commands: Navigate to the login page to verify: http://{trigger.2.host}:{trigger.2.port}{trigger.3.name}
Attempt to log in manually with user '{trigger.1.name}' and password '{trigger.1.attributes.password}'.
For automated testing, inspect the login form's POST request and use a tool like Hydra.

Example Hydra command (YOU MUST CUSTOMIZE THE FORM DATA): hydra -l '{trigger.1.name}' -p '{trigger.1.attributes.password}' {trigger.2.host} http-post-form '/{trigger.3.name}:username=^USER^&password=^PASS^:F=Login Failed'
